title: Platform for enhanced chance-based games with fixed odds payouts
abstract: A system for presenting a chance-based game. The system includes at least one server having a processor configured to execute machine-readable code. The processor is configured to cause the server to create and provide a set of 3D chance-based games to the plurality of presentation devices of the users, which each present the set of 3D chance-based games on a respective display. Each game of the set having an assigned fixed odds payout structure based on winning user selections. The server receives, over the wide area network, data from the presentation devices characterizing a set of user-selected or computer selected winners of the 3D chance-based games. The server updates the event data pertinent to participants in the actual events associated with the 3D advanced choice games. The server uses the updated data to calculate and assign payouts to the users based on the fixed payout structure.
claims: 1. A computer-implemented method comprising: generating one or more matchups for a skill-based game, the skill-based game being configured to allow selection of potential winners from among the one or more matchups, wherein each of the generated matchups includes at least two components associated with at least one participant in one or more real-world events that are selected so as to produce weighted outcomes that are generally equally weighted within the skill-based game, wherein selection of the at least two components for each matchup includes identifying and evaluating a plurality of different real-world, real-time sporting events occurring in different venues performed by different athlete participants at the same time; and wherein the skill-based game provides a prize that is initially available to win by each user that achieves a winning grid pattern without playing a prior skill-based game; placing at least one matchup of the one or more matchups into a first specified position in a grid, wherein the grid comprises a plurality of possible positions and has, associated therewith, a winning grid pattern; receiving at least one selection from a user specifying which of the at least two components in the matchup was selected as a winning component for the matchup; determining that the matchup was a winner, based on which of the at least two components of the matchup was selected by the user, such that the first position of the grid is marked as winning in the grid; determining, in conjunction with at least a second winning matchup, that the grid matches the winning grid pattern; and initializing a lottery selection to award the prize, wherein the lottery selects a specified identifier, and wherein the user and other users that achieved winning grid patterns and matched the lottery-selected identifier are awarded at least a portion of the prize.
